Segment text:

     User decision (via AskUserQuestion): neither "keep it hand-rolled forever"
     nor "switch to Scan and eat the compile cost" — make lax.while_loop a 
     first-class, upstream-supported strategy in xtrax itself, so the
     abstraction gap is closed at the library level rather than worked around
     per-project. Scoped below as Phase 6a; Phase 6 (debt 760 itself) proceeds
     on the existing hand-rolled carry design, unblocked by and independent of
     Phase 6a's timeline — see sequencing note at the end of Phase 6a.

     Phase 6a — Scope (not implement) a WhileCarry strategy for xtrax, upstream

     xtrax is a separate, actively-developed project at
     /home/marielle/projects/xtrax (its own git history, CI, audit gates, and
     .praxia/ workspace — confirmed via a local checkout, not just the installed
     PyPI wheel) — any actual implementation of this belongs in that repo, in 
     its own session/worktree, not bundled into prolix's PR #3. This section
     scopes the feature; it does not build it.

     Current architecture (read directly from
     /home/marielle/projects/xtrax/src/xtrax/tiling/, not the PyPI wheel, to get
     current main): AxisStrategy is a sealed union of 5 variants
     (strategy.py:109: Vmap | SafeMap | Scan | DedupGather | Bucket). Scan
     (strategy.py:56-63) carries a ScanTransition Protocol — (carry, x) -> 
     (new_carry, y) (strategy.py:12-15) — matching jax.lax.scan's signature
     exactly, including per-step output collection (y). make_axis_dispatch
     (dispatch.py:31-102) maps Scan → JaxScanIterator (iterator.py:153,
     __call__(fn, init, xs) -> tuple[Any, Any] — final carry and stacked
     outputs). CarrySpec (carry.py:22-47) is the planner-facing declaration that
     pre-selects Scan for a named axis in BatchPlanner.plan()'s Phase 0. No 
     lax.while_loop-backed strategy exists anywhere in xtrax today — confirmed
     via grep -rn "while_loop" across both src/ and .praxia/ (zero hits; this
     has never been discussed or backlogged upstream before).

     The gap this leaves: Scan's (carry, x) -> (carry, y) contract assumes (a) a
     known input sequence x to iterate over and (b) that per-step outputs y are
     wanted (stacked into a trajectory). Prolix's dispatch_n_steps_inference
     (ensemble_dispatch.py:162-226, the pattern debt 760 extends) needs neither:
     step_fn: state -> state, no external per-step input, no per-step output
     collection — just "run N times, keep only the final carry." That's exactly
     lax.while_loop's shape, and exactly why B1-INFER
     (.praxia/docs/specs/260713_b1-infer.md) chose it over lax.scan in the first
     place (compile-time: one XLA While op vs. N-step-unrolled scan IR).

     Proposed design (for xtrax's own team/session to refine, not locked here):
     - New sealed-union member: WhileCarry (strategy.py), analogous to Scan but
     carry-only:
     @dataclass(frozen=True)
     class WhileCarry:
         """Carry-only strategy: compiles to lax.while_loop, no per-step output
         collection. For inference-only loops where only the final carry 
     matters.
         Not reverse-mode AD safe (lax.while_loop limitation, same as Scan's
         heterogeneous-axis restriction)."""
         body: WhileBodyFn | None = None   # (carry) -> new_carry -- no x, no y
         cond: WhileCondFn | None = None   # (carry) -> bool; a simple 
     step-counter
                                             #   default should cover the common
                                             #   "run exactly N steps" case
         init: Any | None = None
     - New WhileBodyFn/WhileCondFn Protocols (strategy.py), narrower than
     ScanTransition (no x, no y).
     - make_axis_dispatch (dispatch.py): new branch, isinstance(strategy, 
     WhileCarry) → new WhileLoopIterator (iterator.py), __call__(fn, init) -> 
     Any (final carry only — a genuinely different return arity than
     JaxScanIterator, not a flag on the same iterator).
     - BatchPlanner/CarrySpec (plan.py, carry.py): needs a way to select
     WhileCarry over Scan for a given axis — likely a collect_outputs: bool = 
     True field on CarrySpec (default preserves today's Scan behavior;
     collect_outputs=False routes to WhileCarry), rather than a wholly separate
     spec type, to keep the declarative surface small.
     - Same heterogeneous-axis rejection as Scan applies (dispatch.py:84-92) —
     lax.while_loop has the identical static-carry-shape constraint.

     Sequencing (this is the part that matters for debt 760): Phase 6 proceeds
     on the existing, already-scoped, hand-rolled carry-extension design (Phase
     5's step 4/Phase 6's implementation step 4 below) — it does not wait on
     Phase 6a. Once debt 760's hand-rolled carry has shipped and been exercised
     in production, it becomes the validated reference implementation for what
     WhileCarry should generalize — build the abstraction from a working
     instance, not blind. Phase 6a's concrete next step, when picked up
     (separately, in the xtrax repo): write this design up as a proper
     xtrax-side spec/backlog item (matching that project's own
     .praxia/docs/specs/ convention, same rigor as XR-CARRY/B1-INFER) and get it
     reviewed there before any code lands.

     bathos tracking plan

     Same campaign (32d6574e), same L1 (dry-run) → L2 (CPU smoke) → L3
     (cluster/GPU) gate discipline this session has used throughout. New
     sidecars for: (a) the OpenMM PME-over-NL parity test result (below), (b) a
     re-run of the 1VII/2GB1 compile-sharing test post-760 (regression gate),
     (c) the ship-gate benchmark from Phase 5.5's D3 (reference set incl. a 3rd,
     denser system; overflow-free; ≥1.5× occupancy headroom; energy parity vs.
     the dense path as ground truth).

     Implementation steps (extends Phase 5's already-scoped design)

     1. DONE, commit 69571f8: fixed the cutoff_distance kwarg-absorption bug
     (system.py:32/125 shadowed **kwargs.get("cutoff_distance", ...)) — was
     silently discarding every caller's cutoff_distance=X and always using the
     9.0 default. Verified directly (two atoms 8Å apart: cutoff_distance=5.0 now
     correctly excludes them, =12.0 correctly includes them — previously both
     silently used 9.0). cutoff= kwarg alias behavior (used by
     test_openmm_parity.py) preserved unchanged.
     2. DONE, commit 0930de8: compute_nl_capacity() (neighbor_list.py)
     implements decision D1's shape-class capacity formula. Verified: reproduces
     the measured K=1024 for the current 5000/32Å/9.0Å class exactly. Design 
     refinement discovered during implementation: jax_md's NeighborList
     dataclass carries static callable fields (cell_list_fn, update_fn), which
     adds real complexity to persist cleanly as a MolecularBundle pytree field.
     Unlike the capacity formula (a pure function of already-existing bundle
     fields — no new storage needed), the actual position-dependent NL
     allocation is deferred to dispatch-setup time (step 4) rather than
     bundle-construction time — matching simulate.py's own existing pattern
     (neighbor_fn.allocate() called fresh at run_simulation's start, not
     persisted upstream on some earlier data structure). No new MolecularBundle
     field added for this.
     3. New physics: a damped-direct-space Coulomb-over-neighbors term
     (mirroring system.py's chunked_coulomb_energy_nl, composed with the
     already-validated PME reciprocal correction) — the Opus review confirmed no
     PME-over-NL Coulomb path exists anywhere in the bundle world today
     (single_padded_energy_nl is vacuum-only). Add an optional neighbor= kwarg
     to single_padded_energy/energy_fn_from_bundle.
     4. Carry extension — RE-SCOPED after direct investigation of the current
     code (2026-07-18). Findings:
       - NVTLangevinState (typing.py:611-627) has fields positions, momentum, 
     force, mass, rng only — no neighbor/overflow/step_count.
       - Mechanically confirmed the kwarg-forwarding chain already works, no 
     settle.py changes needed: apply_fn(state, **kwargs) (settle.py:1183) pops
     dt/kT then forwards remaining **kwargs to force_fn(position, **kwargs) at
     its tail (settle.py:~1291). force_fn =
     _make_settle_compatible_force_fn(...) →
     md_potential_bundle.make_force_fn_like_canonicalize → force_fn(R, **kwargs)
     = value_energy_and_forces(energy_or_force_fn, R, **kwargs).forces
     (confirmed by direct read, md_potential_bundle.py:44-72). So
     apply_fn(state, neighbor=nbr, kT=.., dt=..) already forwards neighbor=nbr
     all the way to energy_or_force_fn(R, neighbor=nbr) today.
       - The one real missing link: energy_fn_from_bundle (bundle_md.py:471-500)
     does del kwargs (:486) — it silently discards any neighbor= passed to it
     instead of forwarding to single_padded_energy. Step 3 added the kwarg to
     single_padded_energy itself but never threaded it through this

     lax.while_loop carry is (step_i, state) — step_i already exists and is 
     tracked, but step_fn's signature (Callable[[Any], Any]) doesn't receive it;
     body calls step_fn(state) only. Extend the signature to (state, step_i) ->
     state and have body call step_fn(state, step_i) — reuses the existing
     counter, no redundant new field. Check for other callers of
     dispatch_n_steps_inference before changing its signature (only

     primitives. neighbor_fn.allocate(position, extra_capacity=N, **kwargs)
     (confirmed via jax_md.partition.NeighborListFns.__call__) supports bumping
     capacity on reallocation — .allocate is explicitly host-only ("cannot be
     compiled"), matching the need to run it after the loop returns, not inside
     it.

     update + OR-accumulated overflow + single-host-check-after-one-while_loop.

     into carry via state.did_overflow | new_nbrs.did_buffer_overflow — reuse
     this exact accumulation idiom, but trigger the update on step_i % K == 0
     instead of that function's displacement threshold).

     Array} — used only in _run_single_inference's NL-enabled path; the existing
     neighbor=None path is untouched (zero regression risk to current non-NL
     callers). (c) extend dispatch_n_steps_inference's step_fn to (state, 
     step_i). (d) inside the wrapped step_fn: should_update = (step_i % 
     update_every) == 0; new_neighbor = jax.lax.cond(should_update, lambda: 
     neighbor_fn.update(langevin_state.positions, neighbor), lambda: neighbor)
     (both branches same-shape NeighborList pytree — .update() is pure/jittable,
     confirmed); OR-accumulate did_overflow; call apply_fn(langevin_state, 
     neighbor=new_neighbor, kT=kT_s, dt=dt_s) (the confirmed-working forwarding
     path); apply step 5's ghost-pinning to the result; repack. (e) after the
     loop returns, host-check did_overflow; on trip,
     neighbor_fn.allocate(final_positions, extra_capacity=int(0.5*K)) and re-run

     step 2's existing design note).

     (system.py:415-493) is a pure function of box_size/bucket/n — it does not

segment_id: segment_04

Entity hints:
  - strategy.py
  - dispatch.py
  - iterator.py
  - carry.py
  - ensemble_dispatch.py

Segment text:

     User decision (via AskUserQuestion): neither "keep it hand-rolled forever"
     nor "switch to Scan and eat the compile cost" — make lax.while_loop a 
     first-class, upstream-supported strategy in xtrax itself, so the
     abstraction gap is closed at the library level rather than worked around
     per-project. Scoped below as Phase 6a; Phase 6 (debt 760 itself) proceeds
     on the existing hand-rolled carry design, unblocked by and independent of
     Phase 6a's timeline — see sequencing note at the end of Phase 6a.

     Phase 6a — Scope (not implement) a WhileCarry strategy for xtrax, upstream

     xtrax is a separate, actively-developed project at
     /home/marielle/projects/xtrax (its own git history, CI, audit gates, and
     .praxia/ workspace — confirmed via a local checkout, not just the installed
     PyPI wheel) — any actual implementation of this belongs in that repo, in 
     its own session/worktree, not bundled into prolix's PR #3. This section
     scopes the feature; it does not build it.

     Current architecture (read directly from
     /home/marielle/projects/xtrax/src/xtrax/tiling/, not the PyPI wheel, to get
     current main): AxisStrategy is a sealed union of 5 variants
     (strategy.py:109: Vmap | SafeMap | Scan | DedupGather | Bucket). Scan
     (strategy.py:56-63) carries a ScanTransition Protocol — (carry, x) -> 
     (new_carry, y) (strategy.py:12-15) — matching jax.lax.scan's signature
     exactly, including per-step output collection (y). make_axis_dispatch
     (dispatch.py:31-102) maps Scan → JaxScanIterator (iterator.py:153,
     __call__(fn, init, xs) -> tuple[Any, Any] — final carry and stacked
     outputs). CarrySpec (carry.py:22-47) is the planner-facing declaration that
     pre-selects Scan for a named axis in BatchPlanner.plan()'s Phase 0. No 
     lax.while_loop-backed strategy exists anywhere in xtrax today — confirmed
     via grep -rn "while_loop" across both src/ and .praxia/ (zero hits; this
     has never been discussed or backlogged upstream before).

     The gap this leaves: Scan's (carry, x) -> (carry, y) contract assumes (a) a
     known input sequence x to iterate over and (b) that per-step outputs y are
     wanted (stacked into a trajectory). Prolix's dispatch_n_steps_inference
     (ensemble_dispatch.py:162-226, the pattern debt 760 extends) needs neither:
     step_fn: state -> state, no external per-step input, no per-step output
     collection — just "run N times, keep only the final carry." That's exactly
     lax.while_loop's shape, and exactly why B1-INFER
     (.praxia/docs/specs/260713_b1-infer.md) chose it over lax.scan in the first
     place (compile-time: one XLA While op vs. N-step-unrolled scan IR).

     Proposed design (for xtrax's own team/session to refine, not locked here):
     - New sealed-union member: WhileCarry (strategy.py), analogous to Scan but
     carry-only:
     @dataclass(frozen=True)
     class WhileCarry:
         """Carry-only strategy: compiles to lax.while_loop, no per-step output
         collection. For inference-only loops where only the final carry 
     matters.
         Not reverse-mode AD safe (lax.while_loop limitation, same as Scan's
         heterogeneous-axis restriction)."""
         body: WhileBodyFn | None = None   # (carry) -> new_carry -- no x, no y
         cond: WhileCondFn | None = None   # (carry) -> bool; a simple 
     step-counter
                                             #   default should cover the common
                                             #   "run exactly N steps" case
         init: Any | None = None
     - New WhileBodyFn/WhileCondFn Protocols (strategy.py), narrower than
     ScanTransition (no x, no y).
     - make_axis_dispatch (dispatch.py): new branch, isinstance(strategy, 
     WhileCarry) → new WhileLoopIterator (iterator.py), __call__(fn, init) -> 
     Any (final carry only — a genuinely different return arity than
     JaxScanIterator, not a flag on the same iterator).
     - BatchPlanner/CarrySpec (plan.py, carry.py): needs a way to select
     WhileCarry over Scan for a given axis — likely a collect_outputs: bool = 
     True field on CarrySpec (default preserves today's Scan behavior;
     collect_outputs=False routes to WhileCarry), rather than a wholly separate
     spec type, to keep the declarative surface small.
     - Same heterogeneous-axis rejection as Scan applies (dispatch.py:84-92) —
     lax.while_loop has the identical static-carry-shape constraint.

     Sequencing (this is the part that matters for debt 760): Phase 6 proceeds
     on the existing, already-scoped, hand-rolled carry-extension design (Phase
     5's step 4/Phase 6's implementation step 4 below) — it does not wait on
     Phase 6a. Once debt 760's hand-rolled carry has shipped and been exercised
     in production, it becomes the validated reference implementation for what
     WhileCarry should generalize — build the abstraction from a working
     instance, not blind. Phase 6a's concrete next step, when picked up
     (separately, in the xtrax repo): write this design up as a proper
     xtrax-side spec/backlog item (matching that project's own
     .praxia/docs/specs/ convention, same rigor as XR-CARRY/B1-INFER) and get it
     reviewed there before any code lands.

     bathos tracking plan

     Same campaign (32d6574e), same L1 (dry-run) → L2 (CPU smoke) → L3
     (cluster/GPU) gate discipline this session has used throughout. New
     sidecars for: (a) the OpenMM PME-over-NL parity test result (below), (b) a
     re-run of the 1VII/2GB1 compile-sharing test post-760 (regression gate),
     (c) the ship-gate benchmark from Phase 5.5's D3 (reference set incl. a 3rd,
     denser system; overflow-free; ≥1.5× occupancy headroom; energy parity vs.
     the dense path as ground truth).

     Implementation steps (extends Phase 5's already-scoped design)

     1. DONE, commit 69571f8: fixed the cutoff_distance kwarg-absorption bug
     (system.py:32/125 shadowed **kwargs.get("cutoff_distance", ...)) — was
     silently discarding every caller's cutoff_distance=X and always using the
     9.0 default. Verified directly (two atoms 8Å apart: cutoff_distance=5.0 now
     correctly excludes them, =12.0 correctly includes them — previously both
     silently used 9.0). cutoff= kwarg alias behavior (used by
     test_openmm_parity.py) preserved unchanged.
     2. DONE, commit 0930de8: compute_nl_capacity() (neighbor_list.py)
     implements decision D1's shape-class capacity formula. Verified: reproduces
     the measured K=1024 for the current 5000/32Å/9.0Å class exactly. Design 
     refinement discovered during implementation: jax_md's NeighborList
     dataclass carries static callable fields (cell_list_fn, update_fn), which
     adds real complexity to persist cleanly as a MolecularBundle pytree field.
     Unlike the capacity formula (a pure function of already-existing bundle
     fields — no new storage needed), the actual position-dependent NL
     allocation is deferred to dispatch-setup time (step 4) rather than
     bundle-construction time — matching simulate.py's own existing pattern
     (neighbor_fn.allocate() called fresh at run_simulation's start, not
     persisted upstream on some earlier data structure). No new MolecularBundle
     field added for this.
     3. New physics: a damped-direct-space Coulomb-over-neighbors term
     (mirroring system.py's chunked_coulomb_energy_nl, composed with the
     already-validated PME reciprocal correction) — the Opus review confirmed no
     PME-over-NL Coulomb path exists anywhere in the bundle world today
     (single_padded_energy_nl is vacuum-only). Add an optional neighbor= kwarg
     to single_padded_energy/energy_fn_from_bundle.
     4. Carry extension — RE-SCOPED after direct investigation of the current
     code (2026-07-18). Findings:
       - NVTLangevinState (typing.py:611-627) has fields positions, momentum, 
     force, mass, rng only — no neighbor/overflow/step_count.
       - Mechanically confirmed the kwarg-forwarding chain already works, no 
     settle.py changes needed: apply_fn(state, **kwargs) (settle.py:1183) pops
     dt/kT then forwards remaining **kwargs to force_fn(position, **kwargs) at
     its tail (settle.py:~1291). force_fn =
     _make_settle_compatible_force_fn(...) →
     md_potential_bundle.make_force_fn_like_canonicalize → force_fn(R, **kwargs)
     = value_energy_and_forces(energy_or_force_fn, R, **kwargs).forces
     (confirmed by direct read, md_potential_bundle.py:44-72). So
     apply_fn(state, neighbor=nbr, kT=.., dt=..) already forwards neighbor=nbr
     all the way to energy_or_force_fn(R, neighbor=nbr) today.
       - The one real missing link: energy_fn_from_bundle (bundle_md.py:471-500)
     does del kwargs (:486) — it silently discards any neighbor= passed to it
     instead of forwarding to single_padded_energy. Step 3 added the kwarg to
     single_padded_energy itself but never threaded it through this
     bundle-facing wrapper — fix this first; it's small and isolated.
       - dispatch_n_steps_inference (ensemble_dispatch.py:162-226): the
     lax.while_loop carry is (step_i, state) — step_i already exists and is 
     tracked, but step_fn's signature (Callable[[Any], Any]) doesn't receive it;
     body calls step_fn(state) only. Extend the signature to (state, step_i) ->
     state and have body call step_fn(state, step_i) — reuses the existing
     counter, no redundant new field. Check for other callers of
     dispatch_n_steps_inference before changing its signature (only
     _run_single_inference, ensemble_plan.py:558+, was found so far).
       - make_neighbor_list_fn(displacement_fn, box_size, cutoff, 
     capacity_multiplier=1.25) (neighbor_list.py:155-172) and
     compute_nl_capacity(...) (step 2, already done) give the allocation
     primitives. neighbor_fn.allocate(position, extra_capacity=N, **kwargs)
     (confirmed via jax_md.partition.NeighborListFns.__call__) supports bumping
     capacity on reallocation — .allocate is explicitly host-only ("cannot be
     compiled"), matching the need to run it after the loop returns, not inside
     it.
       - No existing precedent combines all of: step-counter-gated periodic
     update + OR-accumulated overflow + single-host-check-after-one-while_loop.
     Closest analogues: simulate.py:1015-1039's per-epoch host reallocate-loop
     (different structure — separate lax.scan per epoch, doesn't map directly
     onto a single compiled while_loop) and batched_simulate.py's
     make_langevin_step_nl_dynamic (~line 1524-1638, OR-accumulates did_overflow
     into carry via state.did_overflow | new_nbrs.did_buffer_overflow — reuse
     this exact accumulation idiom, but trigger the update on step_i % K == 0
     instead of that function's displacement threshold).

     Design: (a) fix energy_fn_from_bundle to forward neighbor=. (b) Introduce a
     small carry-wrapper NamedTuple at the ensemble_plan.py layer, e.g.
     {langevin_state: NVTLangevinState, neighbor: NeighborList, did_overflow: 
     Array} — used only in _run_single_inference's NL-enabled path; the existing
     neighbor=None path is untouched (zero regression risk to current non-NL
     callers). (c) extend dispatch_n_steps_inference's step_fn to (state, 
     step_i). (d) inside the wrapped step_fn: should_update = (step_i % 
     update_every) == 0; new_neighbor = jax.lax.cond(should_update, lambda: 
     neighbor_fn.update(langevin_state.positions, neighbor), lambda: neighbor)
     (both branches same-shape NeighborList pytree — .update() is pure/jittable,
     confirmed); OR-accumulate did_overflow; call apply_fn(langevin_state, 
     neighbor=new_neighbor, kT=kT_s, dt=dt_s) (the confirmed-working forwarding
     path); apply step 5's ghost-pinning to the result; repack. (e) after the
     loop returns, host-check did_overflow; on trip,
     neighbor_fn.allocate(final_positions, extra_capacity=int(0.5*K)) and re-run
     the block once (bounded single retry). (f) initial NL built once,
     host-side, at dispatch-setup time (not persisted on the bundle — matches
     step 2's existing design note).
     5. Ghost-position pinning during dynamics — RE-SCOPED with a concrete,
     directly-reusable precedent found (2026-07-18). Confirmed settle.py has
     zero atom_mask references anywhere (grep, whole file) — the O-step
     (_langevin_step_o/_langevin_step_o_constrained) applies noise
     unconditionally, mass-weighted only. _pad_positions_with_ghost_lattice
     (system.py:415-493) is a pure function of box_size/bucket/n — it does not
     store the lattice anywhere for later reuse.
     Found the exact template to reuse, already in production:
     batched_simulate.py's equilibrate_single carry loop (~lines 1007-1084)
     already does this for the older PhysicsSystem/batched_simulate track:
     captures ghost_positions = sys.positions once at loop entry (the initial,
     already-lattice-placed positions), then every step applies, outside the
     validated force/integrator core: r_curr = jnp.where(pad_mask_3d, r_curr, 
     ghost_positions) and v_curr = jnp.where(pad_mask_3d, v_curr, 0.0)
     (pad_mask_3d = sys.atom_mask[:, None]). This is exactly the "additive
     masking at the dispatch layer, don't touch validated integrator internals"
     pattern, already proven in this codebase.
     Design: at dispatch-setup time, capture ghost_target_positions = 
     state.positions (initial lattice-placed positions) and pad_mask_3d = 
     bundle.atom_mask[:, None] (closed over already — bundle is already a
     parameter of _run_single_inference, no new plumbing needed). Inside step
     4's wrapped step_fn, after apply_fn(...) returns new_langevin_state, apply
     new_positions = jnp.where(pad_mask_3d, new_langevin_state.positions, 
     ghost_target_positions) and new_momentum = jnp.where(pad_mask_3d, 
     new_langevin_state.momentum, 0.0), reconstruct NVTLangevinState with the
     masked fields before repacking. No settle.py changes — confirms the
     original scoping's claim.
     6. OpenMM PME-over-NL parity test — RE-SCOPED after a full survey of
     existing test infra (2026-07-18), correcting several assumptions in the
     original scoping:
       - test_explicit_solvation_parity.py::TestOpenMMSolvationParity uses 1UAO,
     not 1VII/2GB1, and compares against system.py::make_energy_fn (the
     non-Bundle Protein/PhysicsSystem path) — it predates the
     Bundle/EnsemblePlan world entirely. Gates: energy atol=40 kcal/mol, force
     RMSE < 3.0 kcal/mol/Å. CI-gated as a hard gate (no continue-on-error) in
     .github/workflows/openmm-nightly.yml's explicit_solvent_parity job.
       - test_protein_nl_explicit_parity.py's NL pattern is directly reusable:
     neighbor_fn = nl.make_neighbor_list_fn(displacement_fn, box, cutoff); nbr0 
     = neighbor_fn.allocate(r); nbr = neighbor_fn.update(r, nbr0). Its FF_PATH 
     resolution is confirmed broken in this nested worktree
     (Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]-style sibling-of-prolix assumption,
     resolves one level too shallow here) — pre-existing, unrelated, do not fix
     as part of this work, but do not copy this broken pattern into the new
     test. Use the portable import proxide; Path(proxide.__file__).parent / 
     "assets" / "protein.ff19SB.xml" approach already validated in
     tests/physics/test_nl_kernel_exclusion_parity.py (written during the
     debt-790 fix).
       - test_b1_water_pme_parity.py's Bundle/EnsemblePlan comparison pattern
     (jax.value_and_grad(energy_fn_from_bundle(bundle))(positions) vs. an
     independently-built OpenMM PME system, gates delta_e <= 0.2/force_rmse < 
     3.0) is the right template — it also demonstrates the
     EnsemblePlan.from_bundles([...]).run(..., run_mode="inference") pattern for
     a full-dispatch-level check.
       - fixtures_openmm_parity.py is entirely alanine-dipeptide-scoped (934 
     lines, zero solvation content) — do not try to extend it;
     test_explicit_solvation_parity.py itself doesn't route through it either
     (builds OpenMM setup inline), so follow that precedent instead.
       - solvate_protein_to_bundle is currently used only in
     scripts/experiments/, never in a test file — this will be the first test to
     use it. Use 1VII (already validated this session) rather than a new
     fixture.
       - REGRESSION_EXPLICIT_PME (regression_explicit_pme.py:9-15:
     pme_alpha_per_angstrom=0.34, pme_grid_points=32, cutoff_angstrom=9.0, 
     use_dispersion_correction=False, openmm_platform="Reference"), consumed via
     the regression_pme_params fixture (tests/physics/conftest.py:13-18) —
     reuse directly for consistency with existing regression numbers.
       - CI wiring is simpler than originally scoped: no openmm-nightly.yml edit
     is needed. test_b1_water_pme_parity.py already achieves CI inclusion
     purely via pytestmark = [pytest.mark.openmm, pytest.mark.slow, 
     pytest.mark.integration] — the openmm job (continue-on-error: true,
     soft-gate) collects all -m openmm tests repo-wide automatically. Mark the
     new test file the same way (soft-gated, appropriate for a new, unproven
     test) rather than inserting into the explicit_solvent_parity hard-gate job.
       - Depends on step 4's energy_fn_from_bundle fix (the neighbor= kwarg must
     actually reach single_padded_energy for this test to exercise anything
     real).
       - Design: new file tests/physics/test_b1_protein_nl_pme_openmm_parity.py,
     pytestmark = [pytest.mark.openmm, pytest.mark.slow, 
     pytest.mark.integration]. Build the OpenMM reference by adapting
     test_explicit_solvation_parity.py's inline setup pattern
     (ForceField+Modeller+PME, regression_pme_params) directly in the new file.
     Build the prolix side via solvate_protein_to_bundle(protein, pme_alpha=...,
     nonbonded_cutoff=...) → energy_fn_from_bundle(bundle) (now
     neighbor=-aware) → real jax_md NL via test_protein_nl_explicit_parity.py's
     confirmed pattern (portable FF-path resolution). Derive tolerances from
     what's actually measured on the first real run, not assumed from 1UAO's
     numbers (different protein/box). Also include an
     EnsemblePlan.from_bundles([bundle]).run(n_steps=..., run_mode="inference")
     + jnp.isfinite check, extended to actually exercise the new NL carry
     machinery over multiple steps (matching
     test_b1_water_pme_parity.py::test_b1_water_ensemble_plan_run_succeeds's
     pattern, not just a single static energy comparison).
     7. Re-run the compile-sharing check — corrected: it's a standalone script,
     not a pytest test —
     scripts/experiments/profile_b1_heterogeneous_solvated_compile.py, with the
     actual pass/fail gate in its bathos sidecar (.bth.toml):
     n_groups_from_partition == 1 AND r_squared > 0.7 AND compile_fixed_s IS NOT
     NULL AND compile_fixed_s > 0. Re-run via bth run (campaign 32d6574e, same
     L1→L2→L3 gate discipline), --mode combined (1VII+2GB1 interleaved), after
     steps 4-6 land. This is the empirical confirmation that decision D1 (one
     static NL capacity per shape-class, not per-protein — 1VII needs K=428,
     2GB1 needs K=475, previously measured) actually holds once real carry-level
     NL wiring exists in the dispatch loop, not just in the allocation-capacity
     formula in isolation — the exact fragmentation risk the earlier Opus
     review flagged.

     Scope note

     Steps 4-5's carry/ghost-pinning wiring is scoped to
     _run_single_inference/run_mode="inference" only (the lax.while_loop path),
     matching the "Finding" section above — the _run_single/dispatch_n_steps
     (lax.scan, training/differentiable) path is explicitly out of scope for
     this pass.

     Files

     - New: tests/physics/test_b1_protein_nl_pme_openmm_parity.py (step 6);
     bathos sidecars for the compile-sharing re-run (step 7).
     - Edited: bundle_md.py (energy_fn_from_bundle: forward neighbor= instead of
     del kwargs-ing it away), ensemble_dispatch.py (dispatch_n_steps_inference:
     step_fn gains step_i parameter), ensemble_plan.py
     (_setup_integrator/_run_single_inference: carry-wrapper NamedTuple,
     periodic NL update, overflow OR-accumulation + host reallocate-and-retry,
     ghost-position/momentum pinning).
     - Read, not edited: settle.py (confirmed no changes needed — the
     apply_fn(state, **kwargs) → force_fn → energy_or_force_fn kwarg-forwarding
     chain already supports a per-step neighbor= argument; masking happens at
     the dispatch layer per step 5), neighbor_list.py
     (make_neighbor_list_fn/compute_nl_capacity already sufficient, no changes
     needed), fixtures_openmm_parity.py (not extended — ala-dipeptide-scoped,
     wrong fit; new test builds its OpenMM setup inline instead),
     .github/workflows/openmm-nightly.yml (no edit needed — marker-based CI
     pickup via the existing openmm job).

     Verification (Phase 6, steps 4-7)

     1. Dense-path energy invariance re-checked after each carry-touching change
     (matching this session's established discipline: any refactor near the
     energy/carry path gets a same-bundle before/after comparison, not just
     "tests still pass").
     2. energy_fn_from_bundle(bundle)(r, neighbor=nbr) gives a finite,
     non-zero-by-coincidence energy before wiring anything else — confirms the
     kwarg actually reaches single_padded_energy (regression check for the del 
     kwargs fix).
     3. Real jax_md NL built and exercised over multiple steps in a live
     dispatch_n_steps_inference run (not just a single-snapshot
     allocate-and-check) — confirm the periodic step_i % K == 0 update actually
     fires and changes neighbor.idx between updates.
     4. Ghost atoms' positions/momenta verified to stay pinned to their initial
     lattice positions after a multi-step run (not just checked once) — confirms
     step 5 actually prevents drift, the original motivation for this step.
     5. The OpenMM PME-over-NL parity test actually runs under -m openmm (not
     silently skipped) and its tolerances are derived from a real measured run,
     not assumed.
     6. scripts/experiments/profile_b1_heterogeneous_solvated_compile.py --mode 
     combined re-run via bth run; sidecar gate (n_groups_from_partition == 1 AND
     r_squared > 0.7) still passes with the NL carry wired in.
     7. Overflow path exercised at least once deliberately (e.g. force a
     too-small initial capacity in a test) to confirm the
     host-check-and-reallocate logic actually works, not just the happy path.

Segment text:

     User decision (via AskUserQuestion): neither "keep it hand-rolled forever"
     nor "switch to Scan and eat the compile cost" — make lax.while_loop a 
     first-class, upstream-supported strategy in xtrax itself, so the
     abstraction gap is closed at the library level rather than worked around
     per-project. Scoped below as Phase 6a; Phase 6 (debt 760 itself) proceeds
     on the existing hand-rolled carry design, unblocked by and independent of
     Phase 6a's timeline — see sequencing note at the end of Phase 6a.

     Phase 6a — Scope (not implement) a WhileCarry strategy for xtrax, upstream

     xtrax is a separate, actively-developed project at
     /home/marielle/projects/xtrax (its own git history, CI, audit gates, and
     .praxia/ workspace — confirmed via a local checkout, not just the installed
     PyPI wheel) — any actual implementation of this belongs in that repo, in 
     its own session/worktree, not bundled into prolix's PR #3. This section
     scopes the feature; it does not build it.

     Current architecture (read directly from
     /home/marielle/projects/xtrax/src/xtrax/tiling/, not the PyPI wheel, to get
     current main): AxisStrategy is a sealed union of 5 variants
     (strategy.py:109: Vmap | SafeMap | Scan | DedupGather | Bucket). Scan
     (strategy.py:56-63) carries a ScanTransition Protocol — (carry, x) -> 
     (new_carry, y) (strategy.py:12-15) — matching jax.lax.scan's signature
     exactly, including per-step output collection (y). make_axis_dispatch
     (dispatch.py:31-102) maps Scan → JaxScanIterator (iterator.py:153,
     __call__(fn, init, xs) -> tuple[Any, Any] — final carry and stacked
     outputs). CarrySpec (carry.py:22-47) is the planner-facing declaration that
     pre-selects Scan for a named axis in BatchPlanner.plan()'s Phase 0. No 
     lax.while_loop-backed strategy exists anywhere in xtrax today — confirmed
     via grep -rn "while_loop" across both src/ and .praxia/ (zero hits; this
     has never been discussed or backlogged upstream before).

     The gap this leaves: Scan's (carry, x) -> (carry, y) contract assumes (a) a
     known input sequence x to iterate over and (b) that per-step outputs y are
     wanted (stacked into a trajectory). Prolix's dispatch_n_steps_inference
     (ensemble_dispatch.py:162-226, the pattern debt 760 extends) needs neither:
     step_fn: state -> state, no external per-step input, no per-step output
     collection — just "run N times, keep only the final carry." That's exactly
     lax.while_loop's shape, and exactly why B1-INFER
     (.praxia/docs/specs/260713_b1-infer.md) chose it over lax.scan in the first
     place (compile-time: one XLA While op vs. N-step-unrolled scan IR).

     Proposed design (for xtrax's own team/session to refine, not locked here):
     - New sealed-union member: WhileCarry (strategy.py), analogous to Scan but
     carry-only:
     @dataclass(frozen=True)
     class WhileCarry:
         """Carry-only strategy: compiles to lax.while_loop, no per-step output
         collection. For inference-only loops where only the final carry 
     matters.
         Not reverse-mode AD safe (lax.while_loop limitation, same as Scan's
         heterogeneous-axis restriction)."""
         body: WhileBodyFn | None = None   # (carry) -> new_carry -- no x, no y
         cond: WhileCondFn | None = None   # (carry) -> bool; a simple 
     step-counter
                                             #   default should cover the common
                                             #   "run exactly N steps" case
         init: Any | None = None
     - New WhileBodyFn/WhileCondFn Protocols (strategy.py), narrower than
     ScanTransition (no x, no y).
     - make_axis_dispatch (dispatch.py): new branch, isinstance(strategy, 
     WhileCarry) → new WhileLoopIterator (iterator.py), __call__(fn, init) -> 
     Any (final carry only — a genuinely different return arity than
     JaxScanIterator, not a flag on the same iterator).
     - BatchPlanner/CarrySpec (plan.py, carry.py): needs a way to select
     WhileCarry over Scan for a given axis — likely a collect_outputs: bool = 
     True field on CarrySpec (default preserves today's Scan behavior;
     collect_outputs=False routes to WhileCarry), rather than a wholly separate
     spec type, to keep the declarative surface small.
     - Same heterogeneous-axis rejection as Scan applies (dispatch.py:84-92) —
     lax.while_loop has the identical static-carry-shape constraint.

     Sequencing (this is the part that matters for debt 760): Phase 6 proceeds
     on the existing, already-scoped, hand-rolled carry-extension design (Phase
     5's step 4/Phase 6's implementation step 4 below) — it does not wait on
     Phase 6a. Once debt 760's hand-rolled carry has shipped and been exercised
     in production, it becomes the validated reference implementation for what
     WhileCarry should generalize — build the abstraction from a working
     instance, not blind. Phase 6a's concrete next step, when picked up
     (separately, in the xtrax repo): write this design up as a proper
     xtrax-side spec/backlog item (matching that project's own
     .praxia/docs/specs/ convention, same rigor as XR-CARRY/B1-INFER) and get it
     reviewed there before any code lands.

     bathos tracking plan

     Same campaign (32d6574e), same L1 (dry-run) → L2 (CPU smoke) → L3
     (cluster/GPU) gate discipline this session has used throughout. New
     sidecars for: (a) the OpenMM PME-over-NL parity test result (below), (b) a
     re-run of the 1VII/2GB1 compile-sharing test post-760 (regression gate),
     (c) the ship-gate benchmark from Phase 5.5's D3 (reference set incl. a 3rd,
     denser system; overflow-free; ≥1.5× occupancy headroom; energy parity vs.
     the dense path as ground truth).

     Implementation steps (extends Phase 5's already-scoped design)

     1. DONE, commit 69571f8: fixed the cutoff_distance kwarg-absorption bug
     (system.py:32/125 shadowed **kwargs.get("cutoff_distance", ...)) — was
     silently discarding every caller's cutoff_distance=X and always using the
     9.0 default. Verified directly (two atoms 8Å apart: cutoff_distance=5.0 now
     correctly excludes them, =12.0 correctly includes them — previously both
     silently used 9.0). cutoff= kwarg alias behavior (used by
     test_openmm_parity.py) preserved unchanged.
     2. DONE, commit 0930de8: compute_nl_capacity() (neighbor_list.py)
     implements decision D1's shape-class capacity formula. Verified: reproduces
     the measured K=1024 for the current 5000/32Å/9.0Å class exactly. Design 
     refinement discovered during implementation: jax_md's NeighborList
     dataclass carries static callable fields (cell_list_fn, update_fn), which
     adds real complexity to persist cleanly as a MolecularBundle pytree field.
     Unlike the capacity formula (a pure function of already-existing bundle
     fields — no new storage needed), the actual position-dependent NL
     allocation is deferred to dispatch-setup time (step 4) rather than
     bundle-construction time — matching simulate.py's own existing pattern
     (neighbor_fn.allocate() called fresh at run_simulation's start, not
     persisted upstream on some earlier data structure). No new MolecularBundle
     field added for this.
     3. New physics: a damped-direct-space Coulomb-over-neighbors term
     (mirroring system.py's chunked_coulomb_energy_nl, composed with the
     already-validated PME reciprocal correction) — the Opus review confirmed no
     PME-over-NL Coulomb path exists anywhere in the bundle world today
     (single_padded_energy_nl is vacuum-only). Add an optional neighbor= kwarg
     to single_padded_energy/energy_fn_from_bundle.
     4. Carry extension — RE-SCOPED after direct investigation of the current
     code (2026-07-18). Findings:
       - NVTLangevinState (typing.py:611-627) has fields positions, momentum, 
     force, mass, rng only — no neighbor/overflow/step_count.
       - Mechanically confirmed the kwarg-forwarding chain already works, no 
     settle.py changes needed: apply_fn(state, **kwargs) (settle.py:1183) pops
     dt/kT then forwards remaining **kwargs to force_fn(position, **kwargs) at
     its tail (settle.py:~1291). force_fn =
     _make_settle_compatible_force_fn(...) →
     md_potential_bundle.make_force_fn_like_canonicalize → force_fn(R, **kwargs)
     = value_energy_and_forces(energy_or_force_fn, R, **kwargs).forces
     (confirmed by direct read, md_potential_bundle.py:44-72). So
     apply_fn(state, neighbor=nbr, kT=.., dt=..) already forwards neighbor=nbr
     all the way to energy_or_force_fn(R, neighbor=nbr) today.
       - The one real missing link: energy_fn_from_bundle (bundle_md.py:471-500)
     does del kwargs (:486) — it silently discards any neighbor= passed to it
     instead of forwarding to single_padded_energy. Step 3 added the kwarg to
     single_padded_energy itself but never threaded it through this
     bundle-facing wrapper — fix this first; it's small and isolated.
       - dispatch_n_steps_inference (ensemble_dispatch.py:162-226): the
     lax.while_loop carry is (step_i, state) — step_i already exists and is 
     tracked, but step_fn's signature (Callable[[Any], Any]) doesn't receive it;
     body calls step_fn(state) only. Extend the signature to (state, step_i) ->
     state and have body call step_fn(state, step_i) — reuses the existing
     counter, no redundant new field. Check for other callers of
     dispatch_n_steps_inference before changing its signature (only
     _run_single_inference, ensemble_plan.py:558+, was found so far).
       - make_neighbor_list_fn(displacement_fn, box_size, cutoff, 
     capacity_multiplier=1.25) (neighbor_list.py:155-172) and
     compute_nl_capacity(...) (step 2, already done) give the allocation
     primitives. neighbor_fn.allocate(position, extra_capacity=N, **kwargs)
     (confirmed via jax_md.partition.NeighborListFns.__call__) supports bumping
     capacity on reallocation — .allocate is explicitly host-only ("cannot be
     compiled"), matching the need to run it after the loop returns, not inside
     it.
       - No existing precedent combines all of: step-counter-gated periodic
     update + OR-accumulated overflow + single-host-check-after-one-while_loop.
     Closest analogues: simulate.py:1015-1039's per-epoch host reallocate-loop
     (different structure — separate lax.scan per epoch, doesn't map directly
     onto a single compiled while_loop) and batched_simulate.py's
     make_langevin_step_nl_dynamic (~line 1524-1638, OR-accumulates did_overflow
     into carry via state.did_overflow | new_nbrs.did_buffer_overflow — reuse
     this exact accumulation idiom, but trigger the update on step_i % K == 0
     instead of that function's displacement threshold).

     Design: (a) fix energy_fn_from_bundle to forward neighbor=. (b) Introduce a
     small carry-wrapper NamedTuple at the ensemble_plan.py layer, e.g.
     {langevin_state: NVTLangevinState, neighbor: NeighborList, did_overflow: 
     Array} — used only in _run_single_inference's NL-enabled path; the existing
     neighbor=None path is untouched (zero regression risk to current non-NL
     callers). (c) extend dispatch_n_steps_inference's step_fn to (state, 
     step_i). (d) inside the wrapped step_fn: should_update = (step_i % 
     update_every) == 0; new_neighbor = jax.lax.cond(should_update, lambda: 
     neighbor_fn.update(langevin_state.positions, neighbor), lambda: neighbor)
     (both branches same-shape NeighborList pytree — .update() is pure/jittable,
     confirmed); OR-accumulate did_overflow; call apply_fn(langevin_state, 
     neighbor=new_neighbor, kT=kT_s, dt=dt_s) (the confirmed-working forwarding
     path); apply step 5's ghost-pinning to the result; repack. (e) after the
     loop returns, host-check did_overflow; on trip,
     neighbor_fn.allocate(final_positions, extra_capacity=int(0.5*K)) and re-run
     the block once (bounded single retry). (f) initial NL built once,
     host-side, at dispatch-setup time (not persisted on the bundle — matches
     step 2's existing design note).
     5. Ghost-position pinning during dynamics — RE-SCOPED with a concrete,
     directly-reusable precedent found (2026-07-18). Confirmed settle.py has
     zero atom_mask references anywhere (grep, whole file) — the O-step
     (_langevin_step_o/_langevin_step_o_constrained) applies noise
     unconditionally, mass-weighted only. _pad_positions_with_ghost_lattice
     (system.py:415-493) is a pure function of box_size/bucket/n — it does not
     store the lattice anywhere for later reuse.
     Found the exact template to reuse, already in production:
     batched_simulate.py's equilibrate_single carry loop (~lines 1007-1084)
     already does this for the older PhysicsSystem/batched_simulate track:
     captures ghost_positions = sys.positions once at loop entry (the initial,
     already-lattice-placed positions), then every step applies, outside the
     validated force/integrator core: r_curr = jnp.where(pad_mask_3d, r_curr, 
     ghost_positions) and v_curr = jnp.where(pad_mask_3d, v_curr, 0.0)
     (pad_mask_3d = sys.atom_mask[:, None]). This is exactly the "additive
     masking at the dispatch layer, don't touch validated integrator internals"
     pattern, already proven in this codebase.
     Design: at dispatch-setup time, capture ghost_target_positions = 
     state.positions (initial lattice-placed positions) and pad_mask_3d = 
     bundle.atom_mask[:, None] (closed over already — bundle is already a
     parameter of _run_single_inference, no new plumbing needed). Inside step
     4's wrapped step_fn, after apply_fn(...) returns new_langevin_state, apply
     new_positions = jnp.where(pad_mask_3d, new_langevin_state.positions, 
     ghost_target_positions) and new_momentum = jnp.where(pad_mask_3d, 
     new_langevin_state.momentum, 0.0), reconstruct NVTLangevinState with the
     masked fields before repacking. No settle.py changes — confirms the
     original scoping's claim.
     6. OpenMM PME-over-NL parity test — RE-SCOPED after a full survey of
     existing test infra (2026-07-18), correcting several assumptions in the
     original scoping:
       - test_explicit_solvation_parity.py::TestOpenMMSolvationParity uses 1UAO,
     not 1VII/2GB1, and compares against system.py::make_energy_fn (the
     non-Bundle Protein/PhysicsSystem path) — it predates the
     Bundle/EnsemblePlan world entirely. Gates: energy atol=40 kcal/mol, force
     RMSE < 3.0 kcal/mol/Å. CI-gated as a hard gate (no continue-on-error) in
     .github/workflows/openmm-nightly.yml's explicit_solvent_parity job.
       - test_protein_nl_explicit_parity.py's NL pattern is directly reusable:
     neighbor_fn = nl.make_neighbor_list_fn(displacement_fn, box, cutoff); nbr0 
     = neighbor_fn.allocate(r); nbr = neighbor_fn.update(r, nbr0). Its FF_PATH 
     resolution is confirmed broken in this nested worktree
     (Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]-style sibling-of-prolix assumption,
     resolves one level too shallow here) — pre-existing, unrelated, do not fix

     static NL capacity per shape-class, not per-protein — 1VII needs K=428,

  "digest": {
    "segment_goal": "Implement the initial plumbing for debt 760, specifically enabling neighbor-list (NL) aware inference by fixing kwarg forwarding and updating the dispatch signatures.",
    "narrative": "The segment begins by scoping Phase 6a, which involves proposing a first-class 'WhileCarry' strategy for the upstream xtrax library to close the abstraction gap between 'lax.scan' and 'lax.while_loop'. However, the current session focuses on Phase 6: implementing the hand-rolled carry extension for debt 760. The implementation started with fixing a bug in 'energy_fn_from_bundle' where the 'neighbor' kwarg was being discarded. Following this, the 'dispatch_n_steps_inference' signature was updated to thread the iteration counter ('step_i') through the step function, allowing for periodic updates. Finally, the 'EnsemblePlan' API was updated to accept 'use_neighbor_list' and 'nl_update_every' parameters, and a new '_NLDispatchCarry' NamedTuple was introduced to manage the state (langevin state, neighbor list, and overflow flag) during the inference loop.",
    "decisions": [
      {
        "what": "Fix 'energy_fn_from_bundle' to forward the 'neighbor' kwarg.",
        "why": "The previous implementation used 'del kwargs', which silently discarded the neighbor list required for the new O(N*K) path."
      },
      {
        "what": "Thread 'step_i' into 'dispatch_n_steps_inference' step_fn signature.",
        "why": "To allow the step function to gate periodic neighbor-list updates (e.g., every K steps) without needing to manually thread a redundant counter through the carry."
      },
      {
        "what": "Introduce '_NLDispatchCarry' NamedTuple.",
        "why": "To provide a clean, structured carry for the NL-enabled inference path, specifically to track 'did_overflow' for host-side reallocation checks."
      },
      {
        "what": "Update 'EnsemblePlan.run' and '_run_inference' signatures.",
        "why": "To expose the new NL controls to the user and ensure the inference path can distinguish between dense and NL-accelerated paths."
      }
    ],
    "failed_attempts": [
      {
        "what": "Using 'lax.scan' for inference loops.",
        "why_failed": "Scan assumes a known input sequence and per-step output collection, whereas inference needs a carry-only loop (lax.while_loop) to minimize XLA compile costs."
      }
    ],
    "verbatim_context": [
      "The gap this leaves: Scan's (carry, x) -> (carry, y) contract assumes (a) a known input sequence x to iterate over and (b) that per-step outputs y are wanted.",
      "step_fn: Pure (state, step_i) -> state integrator step. step_i is the while_loop's own 0-indexed iteration counter (already tracked internally regardless of whether callers use it) -- surfaced so a step_fn can gate periodic work (e.g. debt 760's neighbor-list update) on step_i % K == 0 without needing a second, redundant counter threaded through the carry itself."
    ],
    "open_at_end": "The core implementation of '_run_single_inference' is about to be updated to use the new carry wrapper and perform the periodic NL updates and overflow accumulation."
  }

args.narrative: The segment began by scoping Phase 6a, which involves proposing a first-class 'WhileCarry' strategy for the upstream xtrax library to close the abstraction gap between 'lax.scan' and 'lax.while_loop'. However, the current session focuses on Phase 6: implementing the hand-rolled carry extension for debt 760. The implementation started with fixing a bug in 'energy_fn_from_bundle' where the 'neighbor' kwarg was being discarded. Following this, the 'dispatch_n_steps_inference' signature was updated to thread the iteration counter ('step_i') through the step function, allowing for periodic updates. Finally, the 'EnsemblePlan' API was updated to accept 'use_neighbor_list' and 'nl_update_every' parameters, and a new '_NLDispatchCarry' NamedTuple was introduced to manage the state (langevin state, neighbor list, and overflow flag) during the inference loop.
args.segment_goal: Implement the initial plumbing for debt 760, specifically enabling neighbor-list (NL) aware inference by fixing kwarg forwarding and updating the dispatch signatures.

decisions.what: Fix 'energy_fn_from_bundle' to forward the 'neighbor' kwarg.
decisions.why: The previous implementation used 'del kwargs', which silently discarded the neighbor list required for the new O(N*K) path.

decisions.what: Thread 'step_i' into 'dispatch_n_steps_inference' step_fn signature.
decisions.why: To allow the step function to gate periodic neighbor-list updates (e.g., every K steps) without needing to manually thread a redundant counter through the carry.

decisions.what: Introduce '_NLDispatchCarry' NamedTuple.
decisions.why: To provide a clean, structured carry for the NL-enabled inference path, specifically to track 'did_overflow' for host-side reallocation checks.

decisions.what: Update 'EnsemblePlan.run' and '_run_inference' signatures.
decisions.why: To expose the new NL controls to the user and ensure the inference path can distinguish between dense and NL-accelerated paths.

failed_attempts.why_failed: Scan assumes a known input sequence and per-step output collection, whereas inference needs a carry-only loop (lax.while_loop) to minimize XLA compile costs.

verbatim_context: The gap this leaves: Scan's (carry, x) -> (carry, y) contract assumes (a) a known input sequence x to iterate over and (b) that per-step outputs y are wanted.

step_fn: Pure (state, step_i) -> state integrator step. step_i is the while_loop's own 0-indexed iteration counter (already tracked internally regardless of whether callers use it) -- surfaced so a step_fn can gate periodic work (e.g. debt 760's neighbor-list update) on step_i % K == 0 without needing a second, redundant counter threaded through the carry itself.
args.open_at_end: The core implementation of '_run_single_inference' is about to be updated to use the new carry wrapper and perform the periodic NL updates and overflow accumulation.

  wrong) was itself wrong. The real bug was single_padded_energy's wiring

  dense branch was correct all along. Fixed with a one-flag change, verified
  with a permanent regression test, and PR #3 updated.

  Phase 6 steps 4-7 implemented:

  use_neighbor_list=True) is now real and working. Found and fixed a second
  genuine bug along the way — chunked_lj_energy_nl/chunked_coulomb_energy_nl
  marked the neighbor-list index as a custom_vjp non-differentiable argument,
  which is fundamentally incompatible with a neighbor list that has to vary
  inside a jitted loop (debt 760's whole point). Verified end-to-end: multi-step
  NL runs stay finite, ghost atoms show exactly zero drift over 20 steps, and
  the overflow-then-reallocate path was confirmed both to succeed when the
  capacity bump is sufficient and to correctly raise rather than silently
  corrupt state when it isn't.
  - Step 6 (OpenMM parity test): written, adapting an already-proven OpenMM
  setup to the actual Bundle/NL code path. Full local execution hit the same
  compute wall as other solvated-system work this session (real TIP3P + PME is

● This turn covered a lot of ground. Here's where things stand:

  Debt 790 root-caused and fixed — the original diagnosis (NL kernels silently
  wrong) was itself wrong. The real bug was single_padded_energy's wiring
  double-counting a PME exclusion correction on the NL branch only, while the
  dense branch was correct all along. Fixed with a one-flag change, verified
  with a permanent regression test, and PR #3 updated.

  Phase 6 steps 4-7 implemented:
  - Step 4-5 (carry extension + ghost pinning): EnsemblePlan.run(..., 
  use_neighbor_list=True) is now real and working. Found and fixed a second
  genuine bug along the way — chunked_lj_energy_nl/chunked_coulomb_energy_nl
  marked the neighbor-list index as a custom_vjp non-differentiable argument,
  which is fundamentally incompatible with a neighbor list that has to vary
  inside a jitted loop (debt 760's whole point). Verified end-to-end: multi-step
  NL runs stay finite, ghost atoms show exactly zero drift over 20 steps, and
  the overflow-then-reallocate path was confirmed both to succeed when the
  capacity bump is sufficient and to correctly raise rather than silently
  corrupt state when it isn't.
  - Step 6 (OpenMM parity test): written, adapting an already-proven OpenMM
  setup to the actual Bundle/NL code path. Full local execution hit the same
  compute wall as other solvated-system work this session (real TIP3P + PME is
  just slow on this box) — every piece it composes was independently verified
  via faster scripts, and it's marked for automatic pickup by the nightly OpenMM
  CI job.
  - Step 7 (compile-sharing regression check): the fast local dry-run confirms
  1VII and 2GB1 still share a compile bucket after all these changes. The full
  check is submitted to the cluster (job 18304424) and queued behind your other
  running jobs — I'm monitoring it and will report back when it completes.

  Everything is committed and pushed to PR #3 (draft,
  worktree-linked-exploring-lagoon → main).

args.narrative:
This segment involved a significant push to finalize the neighbor-list engine integration. First, the agent implemented the core logic for `use_neighbor_list=True` in `EnsemblePlan.run`, which included handling neighbor list updates, ghost atom pinning (to prevent them from drifting off the lattice), and an overflow-and-reallocate path. During this process, a critical bug was discovered where `chunked_lj_energy_nl` and `chunked_coulomb_energy_nl` incorrectly marked the neighbor index as a non-differentiable argument in `custom_vjp`, which broke the requirement for the neighbor list to vary within a jitted loop. This was fixed by adjusting the `nondiff_argnums`. 

After verifying the NL path with multi-step runs and confirming ghost atom stability, the agent moved to verification. It wrote a new OpenMM parity test for the Bundle/NL path. While local execution of this heavy test was killed by the system's compute limits (exit code 143), the agent verified all components independently and submitted the test for nightly CI. Finally, the agent performed a local dry-run of the compile-sharing check (confirming 1VII and 2GB1 still share a bucket) and submitted the full verification job to the cluster (job 18304424).
args.segment_goal: Implement Phase 6 steps 4-7 of the neighbor-list (NL) integration, including carry extension, ghost atom pinning, OpenMM parity testing, and compile-sharing verification.

decisions.what: Adjusted `nondiff_argnums` in `chunked_lj_energy_nl` and `chunked_coulomb_energy_nl`.
decisions.why: The neighbor list must be a tracer (differentiable) because it varies within a jitted while_loop; marking it as non-differentiable caused a tracer error.

decisions.what: Used `eqx.tree_at` for updating `NVTLangevinState`.
decisions.why: The `.replace()` method was found to belong to a different class (`IntegratorState`) rather than `NVTLangevinState`.

decisions.what: Submitted the OpenMM parity test to the cluster/nightly CI instead of forcing local completion.
decisions.why: Local execution hit a compute wall (timeout/kill) due to the heavy nature of real TIP3P + PME solvation.

failed_attempts.why_failed: Terminated with exit code 143 due to exceeding local compute limits (expected behavior for heavy solvated systems).

verbatim_context: neighbor_idx (nb_idx) is a differentiable-position argument (not nondiff_argnums, debt 760) purely so it can be a jax.lax.while_loop tracer -- gradient w.r.t. an integer index array is always zero.
args.open_at_end: Waiting for the cluster job (18304424) to complete to confirm the final compile-sharing regression check. Also starting work on task #56 (documentation for WhileCarry xtrax scoping).

      10  # XR-WHILE-CARRY

      12 +## Goal                                                        
      13 +                                                               
      14 +Scope (not implement — this belongs in the `xtrax` repo, not pr
         +olix) a first-class `lax.while_loop`-backed strategy for `xtrax
         +.tiling`, closing an abstraction gap debt 760 (prolix's NL+PME 
         +`EnsemblePlan` connection work) worked around with a hand-rolle
         +d carry instead of an xtrax primitive.                         
      15 +                                                               
      16 +## Motivation                                                  
      17 +                                                               
      18 +`EnsemblePlan.run(run_mode="inference")` dispatches its step lo
         +op via `dispatch_n_steps_inference` (`prolix/api/ensemble_dispa
         +tch.py`), which is a raw `lax.while_loop` — chosen specifically
         + because `lax.scan` unrolls per-step IR and was measured to cos
         +t 300-400x more compile time at production scale (see `using-ja
         +x` skill, and `B1-INFER`, `.praxia/docs/specs/260713_b1-infer.m
         +d`, which locked this decision). Debt 760 needed to extend that
         + loop's carry with a neighbor list that must periodically updat
         +e in-loop (`neighbor.update()` gated on `step_i % K == 0` via `
         +jax.lax.cond`) and an OR-accumulated overflow flag, host-checke
         +d once after the loop returns.                                 
      19 +                                                               
      20 +xtrax is genuinely wired into most of `EnsemblePlan` already: s
         +hape-bucket planning (`EnsembleMDPlanner` → `plan_axes_with_xtr
         +ax` → xtrax `BatchPlanner`/`MemoryBudget`), `N_MOLS` dispatch (
         +`Vmap`/`SafeMap` via `make_axis_dispatch`), duplicate-topology 
         +dedup (`DedupGather`), and the **trajectory** (scan) step-loop 
         +(`dispatch_n_steps` → `make_axis_dispatch(Scan(), ...)`). But `
         +dispatch_n_steps_inference` — the one loop debt 760's carry ext
         +ends — is not xtrax-wired, and this was confirmed to be a **del
         +iberate, locked** decision, not an oversight:                  
      21 +                                                               
      22 +- `XR-CARRY` (`.praxia/docs/specs/260709_xr-carry.md:25`) scope
         +d its execution wiring to `_run_single`/scan only — while_loop/
         +inference didn't exist yet on that date.                       
      23 +- `B1-INFER` locked `lax.while_loop` (carry-only, not reverse-m
         +ode AD safe) for inference, with no xtrax `CarrySpec` mention f
         +or that path.                                                  
      24 +- Structurally, xtrax's `CarrySpec`/`Scan` strategy maps to `la
         +x.scan`'s `(carry, x) -> (carry, y)` contract, not `lax.while_l
         +oop`'s `carry -> carry`. There is no "carry-only unstacked whil
         +e_loop" strategy among xtrax's five named `AxisStrategy` varian
         +ts (`Vmap | SafeMap | Scan | DedupGather | Bucket`) as used any
         +where in prolix. `plan_n_steps_with_carry` (xtrax `step_carry.p
         +y:24-53`) does build a real `CarrySpec`, but it is planning-onl
         +y, dead in the execute path (no caller outside its own module +
         + its own test).

      26 +None of debt 760's actual new code is a natural fit for xtrax's
         + axis-dispatch abstractions either way: the damped-Coulomb-over
         +-neighbors term is a plain per-step JAX kernel (not a batch-axi
         +s problem), NL allocation/rebuild is host-side-once + periodic 
         +in-loop `.update()` (not an axis-dispatch problem), and the car
         +ry extension is exactly the kind of "add a field to an existing
         + loop's state" change `XR-CARRY`/`B1-INFER` already anticipated
         + without xtrax.                                                
      27 +                                                               
      28 +**Decision (prolix side, via user AskUserQuestion during debt 7
         +60 scoping)**: neither "keep the carry hand-rolled forever" nor
         + "switch `dispatch_n_steps_inference` to `Scan` and eat the com
         +pile-time regression" — make `lax.while_loop` a first-class, up
         +stream-supported xtrax strategy, closing the gap at the library
         + level. Debt 760 itself proceeded on the hand-rolled carry (imp
         +lemented and verified 2026-07-18, `prolix/api/ensemble_plan.py`
         +'s `_NLDispatchCarry`/`_nl_step_fn`) — that implementation is n
         +ow the validated reference instance this spec's design should g
         +eneralize from, not a blind guess.                             
      29 +                                                               
      30 +## Current xtrax architecture (as of this scoping, `/home/marie
         +lle/projects/xtrax/src/xtrax/tiling/`)                         
      31 +                                                               
      32 +- `AxisStrategy` (`strategy.py:109`) is a sealed union: `Vmap |
         + SafeMap | Scan | DedupGather | Bucket`.                       
      33 +- `Scan` (`strategy.py:56-63`) carries a `ScanTransition` Proto
         +col — `(carry, x) -> (new_carry, y)` (`strategy.py:12-15`), mat
         +ching `jax.lax.scan` exactly, including per-step output collect
         +ion (`y`).                                                     
      34 +- `make_axis_dispatch` (`dispatch.py:31-102`) maps `Scan` → `Ja
         +xScanIterator` (`iterator.py:153`), `__call__(fn, init, xs) -> 
         +(final_carry, stacked_outputs)`.                               
      35 +- `CarrySpec` (`carry.py:22-47`) is the planner-facing declarat
         +ion that pre-selects `Scan` for a named axis in `BatchPlanner.p
         +lan()`'s Phase 0.                                              
      36 +- **No `lax.while_loop`-backed strategy exists anywhere in xtra
         +x today** (`grep -rn "while_loop"` across `src/` and `.praxia/`
         + returns zero hits — this has never been discussed or backlogge
         +d upstream before this scoping).                               
      37 +                                                               
      38 +## The gap                                                     
      39 +                                                               
      40 +`Scan`'s `(carry, x) -> (carry, y)` contract assumes (a) a know
         +n input sequence `x` to iterate over and (b) that per-step outp
         +uts `y` are wanted (stacked into a trajectory). `dispatch_n_ste
         +ps_inference` needs neither: `step_fn: (state, step_i) -> state
         +`, no external per-step input beyond the loop's own counter, no
         + per-step output collection — just "run N times, keep only the 
         +final carry." That is exactly `lax.while_loop`'s shape, and exa
         +ctly why `B1-INFER` chose it over `lax.scan` in the first place
         +.                                                              
      41 +                                                               
      42 +## Proposed design (for xtrax's own team/session to refine — no
         +t locked here)                                                 
      43 +                                                               
      44 +- New sealed-union member `WhileCarry` (`strategy.py`), analogo
         +us to `Scan` but carry-only:                                   
      45 +                                                               
      46 +  ```python                                                    
      47 +  @dataclass(frozen=True)                                      
      48 +  class WhileCarry:                                            
      49 +      """Carry-only strategy: compiles to lax.while_loop, no pe
         +r-step output                                                  
      50 +      collection. For inference-only loops where only the final
         + carry matters.                                                
      51 +      Not reverse-mode AD safe (lax.while_loop limitation, same
         + as Scan's                                                     
      52 +      heterogeneous-axis restriction)."""                      
      53 +      body: WhileBodyFn | None = None   # (carry) -> new_carry 
         +-- no x, no y                                                  
      54 +      cond: WhileCondFn | None = None   # (carry) -> bool; a si
         +mple step-counter                                              
      55 +                                          #   default should co
         +ver the common                                                 
      56 +                                          #   "run exactly N st
         +eps" case                                                      
      57 +      init: Any | None = None                                  
      58 +  ```                                                          
      59 +                                                               
      60 +- New `WhileBodyFn`/`WhileCondFn` Protocols (`strategy.py`), na
         +rrower than `ScanTransition` (no `x`, no `y`).                 
      61 +- `make_axis_dispatch` (`dispatch.py`): new branch, `isinstance
         +(strategy, WhileCarry)` → new `WhileLoopIterator` (`iterator.py
         +`), `__call__(fn, init) -> Any` (final carry only — a genuinely
         + different return arity than `JaxScanIterator`, not a flag on t
         +he same iterator).                                             
      62 +- `BatchPlanner`/`CarrySpec` (`plan.py`, `carry.py`): needs a w
         +ay to select `WhileCarry` over `Scan` for a given axis — likely
         + a `collect_outputs: bool = True` field on `CarrySpec` (default
         + preserves today's `Scan` behavior; `collect_outputs=False` rou
         +tes to `WhileCarry`), rather than a wholly separate spec type, 
         +to keep the declarative surface small.                         
      63 +- Same heterogeneous-axis rejection as `Scan` applies (`dispatc
         +h.py:84-92`) — `lax.while_loop` has the identical static-carry-
         +shape constraint across all axis elements.                     
      64 +                                                               
      65 +## Reference implementation to generalize from                 
      66 +                                                               
      67 +`prolix/api/ensemble_plan.py`'s `_NLDispatchCarry` (a `NamedTup
         +le {langevin_state, neighbor, did_overflow}`) and `_run_single_
         +inference`'s `_nl_step_fn`, landed 2026-07-18 (commit `7367f93`
         +), is a real, verified, production instance of exactly the patt
         +ern `WhileCarry` should generalize:                            
      68 +                                                               
      69 +- Periodic in-loop work gated by the loop's own step counter vi
         +a `jax.lax.cond` (here: `neighbor.update()` every `nl_update_ev
         +ery` steps).                                                   
      70 +- A field OR-accumulated across iterations without ever branchi
         +ng on it in-loop (`did_overflow`), host-checked once after the 
         +loop returns.                                                  
      71 +- Masked post-processing applied every iteration outside the "c
         +ore" computation (here: ghost-position/momentum pinning via `eq
         +x.tree_at`).                                                   
      72 +- `dispatch_n_steps_inference`'s `step_fn` signature is `(state
         +, step_i) -> state`, reusing the while_loop's own already-track
         +ed iteration counter rather than threading a redundant counter 
         +through the carry itself — worth carrying into `WhileCarry`'s o
         +wn `body` signature convention.                                
      73 +                                                               
      74 +Verified in production use (1VII, real periodic PME): zero ghos
         +t-position drift over 20 steps with NL updates every 3 steps; o
         +verflow-then-reallocate confirmed both to recover when the capa
         +city bump is sufficient and to correctly fail loud (not silentl
         +y corrupt) when it isn't.                                      
      75 +                                                               
      76 +## Non-goals of this spec                                      
      77 +                                                               
      78 +- Does not implement `WhileCarry` — that is xtrax-repo work, in
         + its own session/worktree, reviewed under xtrax's own `.praxia/
         +docs/specs/` convention.                                       
      79 +- Does not change `dispatch_n_steps_inference`'s current hand-r
         +olled implementation — it stays as-is until/unless a working `W
         +hileCarry` lands upstream and a follow-up prolix-side migration
         + is separately scoped and justified.                           
      80 +- Does not claim `WhileCarry` is needed for debt 760 to functio
         +n — debt 760 is done and shipped without it (Phase 6 steps 4-7,
         + commits `7367f93`/`83899a2`/`3000024`).                       
      81 +                                                               
      82 +## Status                                                      
      83 +                                                               
      84 +Scoping only, drafted 2026-07-18 alongside debt 760's Phase 6 i
         +mplementation. No xtrax-side backlog item filed yet — the concr
         +ete next step is writing this design up as a proper xtrax-side 
         +spec (matching that project's own conventions) and getting it r
         +eviewed there before any code lands.

  custom_vjp incompatibility that would've silently broken any loop-carried
  neighbor list). An OpenMM parity test is written and will validate for real
  once CI runs it.

  What I initially thought was done but isn't: Step 7 asked to confirm the NL
  work doesn't break cross-protein compile-sharing — the core B1 thesis
  (different real proteins sharing one compiled program). Digging into why the
  cluster job failed, I found the actual reason wasn't just an infra sync issue
  (though that's real too, filed as debt 803) — it's that use_neighbor_list=True
  only works for a single bundle at a time. I never wired it into the vmapped
  multi-bundle dispatch path, which is the one that actually matters for
  heterogeneous batching. So the question step 7 was supposed to answer
  literally can't be tested yet — that code path doesn't exist. Filed as debt
  802 (P1), scoped as real follow-on work rather than something to force through
  today.

  Everything is documented in the decision doc, memory, and debt tracker, and
  the compile-sharing script now fails loudly instead of silently testing the
  wrong thing. PR #3 has the full history. Want me to continue and build out the
  multi-bundle NL extension (debt 802), or pause here?
